answers.value:
Requesting expedited review due to rapidly worsening functional status and high fall risk in a frail 76-year-old Medicare Advantage member.

Over the past 6 months, Ms. Martha Henderson has experienced a marked decline in mobility and independence secondary to severe osteoarthritis of the left knee. She now reports that she can walk only about half a block, even with a cane, before needing to stop because of intense pain and feeling that the knee may "give out." She has had two documented falls in the last three months:

1) On 2024-09-18, she fell while stepping off a curb near her home when her left knee buckled. She sustained a significant left forearm hematoma and abrasions but fortunately no fracture. This event is documented in my clinic note dated 2024-09-20 in the Providence EMR.

2) On 2024-11-02, she slipped in her kitchen while turning and reported that her left knee "locked" and then gave out. Although she did not sustain a hip fracture, she had extensive bruising over her left hip and required assistance from a neighbor and EMS lift assist to get up. Since that event, she has become extremely fearful of falling again and has started limiting her activity to the bare minimum.

Conservative treatment has been fully exhausted and is no longer effective. Over the last 18 months, she has undergone:
- Multiple courses of physical therapy focused on quadriceps strengthening and gait training (most recently from 2024-04 through 2024-07), with only transient mild improvement.
- Regular use of acetaminophen, topical diclofenac, and intermittent low-dose NSAIDs, which now provide minimal relief and are limited by her mild chronic kidney disease and history of gastritis.
- Two intra-articular corticosteroid injections (2024-02-10 and 2024-08-15) with diminishing returns — the last injection provided only about two weeks of partial symptom relief.

Her most recent weight-bearing radiographs from 2024-10-05 show severe tricompartmental osteoarthritis of the left knee with joint space narrowing to bone-on-bone contact in the medial compartment, osteophyte formation, and subchondral sclerosis. The orthopedic surgeon, Dr. Laura J. McMillan, has determined that she is an appropriate candidate for elective total knee arthroplasty and that further delay will not provide additional benefit from conservative measures.

Functionally, she is now struggling with basic activities of daily living:
- She has difficulty safely navigating stairs to the laundry area in her basement and has nearly fallen multiple times.
- She avoids showering on some days because stepping into the tub feels unstable, raising concern for a potentially catastrophic fall.
- She can no longer stand long enough to cook full meals, leading her to rely more on processed and microwave foods, which is negatively impacting her nutritional status and weight.

Given her age, comorbidities, and documented history of recent falls, additional delays in surgical intervention significantly increase her risk of a serious fall-related injury such as a hip fracture or head trauma. Such outcomes would likely require prolonged hospitalization, rehabilitation, and potentially long-term institutional care, which would be far more costly and detrimental to her quality of life than proceeding with timely knee replacement surgery.

For these reasons, I am requesting expedited review so that we can proceed with surgery on or near 2025-01-20 as scheduled, minimizing the window of time in which she remains at high risk for a disabling fall and further functional decline.

answers.value:
All requested services, including the orthopedic consultation, planned total knee arthroplasty, inpatient hospital stay, and subsequent outpatient physical therapy, are to be performed within the Providence network. The requesting provider (Dr. Daniel R. Kim), the orthopedic surgeon (Dr. Laura J. McMillan), and the servicing facility (Providence Portland Medical Center) are all contracted in-network providers/facilities with Providence Medicare Advantage. There is no need for out-of-network coverage for this case, and no out-of-network providers are being requested.

This authorization request is being submitted to ensure appropriate pre-approval for the in-network inpatient admission, surgical procedure, related imaging, and a defined course of post-operative physical therapy visits under the member’s current Providence Medicare Advantage plan.
